items.title: Canadian mining company Sherritt International halts plans to dissolve Cuba business amid sanctions
items.summary_auto: The company says it stopped the dissolution plan after consultations with its advisers, stakeholders and relevant governmental authorities, reversing a decision it announced last week as the U.S. expands sanctions on the country.

items.title: Married at First Sight UK pulls episodes after brides allege sexual assault by co-stars
items.summary_auto: Three female contestants from the matchmaking reality show Married at First Sight UK have come forward with allegations that they were sexually assaulted by their on-screen partners.

items.title: Vancouver Canucks fire head coach Adam Foote
items.summary_auto: Assistant coaches Scott Young, Kevin Dean and Brett McLean have also been dismissed as the team continues its makeover.

items.title: San Diego mosque shooters met online and shared hateful views, FBI says
items.summary_auto: U.S. federal authorities say the teenagers who killed three people at a San Diego mosque met online and shared a "broad hatred" toward different religions and races.

items.title: Alberta's timeline for West Coast pipeline 'best-case scenario': CIBC analysts
items.summary_auto: Analysts are encouraged by a "sense of urgency" surrounding a potential new pipeline, but point out the provincial and federal governments still need to agree on the Pathways carbon capture project. There is also opposition to the project from B.C. 's government and some Indigenous groups.

items.title: Heating and cooling buildings is heating the planet, UN says. Here are some solutions
items.summary_auto: Buildings spew nearly 40 per cent of the world's planet-warming emissions and the amount is growing, says a new UN report. That's partly because fossil fuels still dominate heating and cooling. Here are some solutions from Kenya to Germany to Colombia, and what Canadians could learn.

items.title: Alberta RCMP officer accused of sexual assault pleads guilty to common assault on teen boys
items.summary_auto: Alberta RCMP Const. Bridget Morla, who was charged with sexually assaulting two teenage boys, has pleaded guilty to one count of common assault.

items.title: Jackson Pollock drip painting soars to record $181.2M US sale at auction
items.summary_auto: A Jackson Pollock artwork highlighting the artist's famed painted swoops, swirls, pours and drizzles soared to an eye-popping sales record at a New York auction Monday evening, fetching $181.2 million US.

items.title: Israeli forces fired shots at Gaza aid flotilla vessels, video shows
items.summary_auto: Israeli forces opened fire on at least two vessels in an aid flotilla sailing towards Gaza on Tuesday, according to video footage and flotilla organizers. But Israel said no live ammunition was used and there were no casualties.

items.title: Where and why Stellantis plans to produce small, low-cost E-Cars
items.summary_auto: The vehicles will draw on partnerships to keep costs down and speed up development, according to the international automaker.

items.title: Former Woodstock, Ont., mayor sentenced to 4 years, 8 months in prison for sexual assaults
items.summary_auto: Former Woodstock mayor Trevor Birtch was sentenced to four years and eight months in prison Tuesday for two sexual assault convictions in a case the judge said represents a “catastrophic fall from grace.”

items.title: Carlos Alcaraz withdraws from Wimbledon in June due to lingering wrist injury
items.summary_auto: Two-time Wimbledon champion Carlos Alcaraz on Tuesday said he is pulling out of the grass-court Grand Slam event next month because of his lingering wrist injury.

items.title: Beloved Montreal café celebrates 70 years of serving community in Little Italy
items.summary_auto: Caffè Italia in Montreal’s Little Italy neighbourhood has been a family affair for four generations. Those behind and in front of the counter tell CBC about the love that’s gone into the business and the love it gives back.

items.title: Calgary police charge high school coach with sexual assault after alleged abuse of minor for nearly 2 years
items.summary_auto: Drew Alan Robertson, 50, has been charged after allegedly using his position of trust as a volunteer coach at Henry Wise Wood High School in Calgary to groom a 15-year-old and sexually abuse her over the course of nearly two years, police say.

items.title: Canadian swim star McIntosh deep into work with legendary coach, aimed toward 5 gold medals at L.A. Olympics
items.summary_auto: It's head down and full steam ahead for Summer McIntosh as she continues her pursuit of five individual gold medals at the 2028 L.A. Olympics.

items.title: Robert K. Irving, prominent Canadian businessman who led J.D. Irving Ltd. and Cavendish Farms, dead at 71
items.summary_auto: A leading member of New Brunswick's Irving family has died. Robert K. Irving, who was co-CEO of J.D. Irving Ltd., died Tuesday.

items.title: Churchill Falls MOU 'not in the public interest,' says N.L. independent review committee
Canada's annual inflation rate rose to 2.8% in April, thanks to soaring energy prices
items.summary_auto: Cost of gasoline was 28.6 per cent higher in April year-over-year, helping boost overall inflation, while the pace of inflation slowed for other costs like rent and food. Economists noted that core measures of inflation that strip out more volatile prices have risen at a much slower clip.

items.title: Todd Blanche grilled over Trump administration's $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund
items.summary_auto: Todd Blanche, acting attorney general in Donald Trump's administration, defended Justice Department plans to create a $1.8 billion US fund that could pay allies of the Republican president who believe they were targeted politically.

items.title: 'Not a shred' of evidence connects Dean Penney to wife's disappearance, defence tells murder trial
WHO concerned about 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak, with 131 now dead
items.summary_auto: At least 131 deaths and over 500 suspected cases have been reported in the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, the Congolese health ministry said Tuesday as the World Health Organization's head expressed concern over the "scale and speed of the epidemic."

items.title: Changes to daycare funding in Sask. prompt some providers to drop part-time spots
items.summary_auto: Upcoming changes to how subsidized child care is funded in Saskatchewan are causing some providers to notify families that they can't provide spaces for their children anymore — particularly for young kids attending on a part-time or drop-in basis.

items.title: How should Canada handle Alberta separatism?
items.summary_auto: A conversation about separatist movements, and what makes them legitimate in the eyes of the law, public and history.

items.title: 'It's appalling': Halifax women condemn 15-month wait for mammograms
items.summary_auto: Cynthia McCutcheon thought she heard wrong when she was told the next available appointment for a mammogram in the Halifax area was in July 2027. She's worried some cancers will be diagnosed at later stages because of the backlog in Nova Scotia.

items.title: Canada's updated its plant hardiness map — and growers are seeing their options bloom
items.summary_auto: Last year, Natural Resources Canada updated its map of "plant hardiness zones" in the wake of the changing climate. Gardeners may have more information this year about which plants will thrive and survive.

items.title: Montreal F1 stripper strike part of fight to legitimize and decriminalize sex work
items.summary_auto: Montreal strippers say that for too long, their employment as independent contractors has shielded strip club owners from ensuring safe working conditions. They're striking on Saturday in a show of collective action unprecedented in recent Canadian history.

items.title: Ontario's claims of economic impact for Toronto Island airport expansion based on incomplete study
items.summary_auto: The Ontario government says expending Billy Bishop airport could provide an $8.5-billion annual economic boost by 2050. But weeks after first touting that claim, neither the province nor the Toronto Port Authority, which owns the airport, has provided any evidence to support it. Several experts say they're skeptical.

items.title: Trump is gunning for Republican Thomas Massie, who defied him on Epstein files
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is yet again aiming to show he has an iron grip on the Republican Party, pushing to oust the Republican congressman who stood up to him on the Epstein files.

items.title: At 'crucial' moment for pandemic preparedness, experts say world isn't ready
items.summary_auto: Government funding cutbacks and a distrust in public health authorities means the world is not ready for the next pandemic, according to a report released Monday by the Global Preparedness Monitoring Board (GPMB).

items.title: My service dog has given me my life back. Distracting him can take that away
items.summary_auto: Rachel Lyons’s service dog, Geneva, helps her navigate the world, and often garners attention from strangers. But when people try to distract Geneva, it can cause medical issues for her.
